steps.title: Solve the unknown directly
steps.content: For infinitely many solutions, the left and right sides must be the same linear expression for every $x$. That means both the $x$-coefficients and the constant terms must match.

steps.title: Verify both identity conditions
steps.content: Algebra gives the same result. Match $x$-coefficients: $$2k+5=k+36$$ $$k=31$$ Match constants: $$3k-16=k+46$$ $$2k=62$$ $$k=31$$ Both parts agree, so the equation becomes identical on both sides only when $k=31$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Substitute $k=31$: the left side is $(67)x+(77)$ and the right side is also $(67)x+(77)$. Therefore the equation has infinitely many solutions, and the answer is 31.
